# Resume a Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode session in one click

> Paneflow finds every agent session tied to your current project and brings any of them back, history intact, without hunting for the right command.

Canonical: https://paneflow.dev/blog/agent-sessions

<!-- Auto-generated mirror of paneflow.dev — do not edit by hand.
     Source of truth: the paneflow.dev docs. Regenerated by
     scripts/sync-public-docs.ts in the site repo. -->

Closing an agent terminal does not delete the session, but finding it again usually means remembering the right flag and the right identifier. Paneflow surfaces the sessions tied to your current project and resumes any of them in one click.

## Sessions scoped to the right project [#sessions-scoped-to-the-right-project]

Paneflow detects the Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode sessions associated with your active working directory. They are grouped by agent, with nothing mixed in from your other projects.

Pick a session and Paneflow resumes it in the current pane, history intact.

## Come back to a task without starting over [#come-back-to-a-task-without-starting-over]

A session holds the exchanges, the decisions, and the plan you built with the agent. Being able to leave and return means you switch between tasks without losing that context or explaining everything from scratch.

Close a pane, restart Paneflow, or step away and come back later. The sessions you need are still there when you want them.

## Start your next session in Paneflow [#start-your-next-session-in-paneflow]

Agent sessions ship in Paneflow 0.3.6. Download the latest build, run an agent inside a project, and find its session waiting for you in Paneflow.
